user_query:
Build me a local, text-based contacts knowledge base from the inputs. Read these files under input/:
- contacts_seed.json (initial contacts with birthdays, tags, interests, family, communication prefs, and past interactions)
- interactions.txt (new interactions to log, one per line: YYYY-MM-DD | Full Name | Note)
- updates.yaml (field updates and additions per person)
- meetings.csv (upcoming meetings with date,time,attendees JSON array,topic)
- reference_date.txt (an ISO date like 2026-04-15 that you must use for time-window calculations)

Create the following under output/:
1) output/contacts/people/{slug}.md — one Markdown file per person. Slug rule: lowercase, strip apostrophes, replace any non-alphanumeric with hyphens, collapse repeats, trim hyphens. Each file must start with YAML frontmatter containing at least: name, birthday (YYYY-MM-DD if known), and tags (array). Then include these sections, in this order, using these exact headings:
- ## Basics (how we met, company, location, communication preference)
- ## Personal details (family members, preferences, etc.)
- ## Interaction history (reverse-chronological; put the newest interactions from interactions.txt at the top, each line as "YYYY-MM-DD: note")
- ## Notes (freeform)
- ## Interests (bullet list)

2) output/contacts/index.md — an alphabetical index by person name that lists each contact with a Markdown link to people/{slug}.md, their tags, and their most recent interaction date.

3) output/contacts/birthdays.md — a list of upcoming birthdays within the next 7 days from the reference date, excluding the reference date itself and including the end day. Include a title line that mentions the reference date (e.g., "# Upcoming Birthdays (next 7 days from 2026-04-15)") and bullet lines formatted as "- MM-DD: Full Name". Do not include birthdays outside the window.

4) output/meetings/reminders.md — meeting prep notes for only those meetings occurring within the next 3 days from the reference date (inclusive). For each such meeting, add a section formatted exactly:
"## YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM - {topic}"
"Attendees: Name1, Name2, ..."
Then, for each attendee, include a line:
"- Name: Last interaction: {YYYY-MM-DD - note}" or "- Name: No previous interactions logged" if none exist. If the attendee has any noted preferences (e.g., vegetarian), include an additional line immediately after their last interaction line: "  Preferences: {comma-separated prefs}".

Apply updates from updates.yaml to the relevant contacts (create the contact if it doesn’t exist yet), merge interests without duplicates, append kids to existing lists if instructed, and add notes as requested. Log all new interactions from interactions.txt into the appropriate person’s file, keeping reverse-chronological order. Create new contacts for any names that appear in interactions or meetings but aren’t in the seed.

Important constraints:
- Use only the provided input files; do not fabricate data.
- Keep dates and windows strictly relative to input/reference_date.txt.
- Ensure frontmatter birthdays for people provided in contacts_seed.json match the seed values exactly.
- Follow the exact headings and file layout specified above.

When done, ensure all outputs are written under output/ with the exact paths described.
